scope: Add IntegratedDisplaySchedulerLoadRecord as first-class integrated scenario evidence
Tie G12 FramebufferBenchmark and P5 SchedulerDecision evidence into one generation-checked display-under-load scenario

Require exact generations for FramebufferBenchmark, SchedulerDecision, Store, RunnableQueue, DisplayObject, FramebufferObject, DisplayCapability, FramebufferWrite, FramebufferFlushRegion, and DisplayEventLog
Derive display sample frames, sample bytes, measured nanos, and scheduler load units from semantic benchmark and scheduler evidence
Reject missing, stale, unrecorded, over-budget, or ordering-invalid display/scheduler evidence
Emit IntegratedDisplaySchedulerLoadRecorded event and stable osctl ViewV1 JSON
Expose historical graph edges without creating live ownership or adapter-internal semantic truth
Publish target_executor semantic package roots and count validation for X5 evidence
no_goals: new graphics or scheduler third-party dependency

implemented_contract_clauses.clause: X5 display update during scheduler load
all integrated display/scheduler load references carry kind/id/generation
integrated scenario uses Historical graph edges for display benchmark and scheduler decision attribution
osctl exposes stable ViewV1 JSON and contract validation output

findings_fixed: contract graph models IntegratedDisplaySchedulerLoad as exact-generation integrated evidence
contract graph validates the scheduler decision root instead of treating task or activation current generations as the source of truth for historical attribution
target_executor writes X5 records, roots, and counts into the semantic package
osctl command, inspect, replay, and graph history expose integrated-display-scheduler-load evidence through stable ViewV1 JSON
semantic validation rejects stale scheduler decision generations and missing display benchmark evidence
bad_smells_checked: No display adapter internal state became semantic truth
No real display hardware execution is claimed
No real preemptive scheduler execution is claimed
No adapter internal structs are serialized as osctl stable views
No target-side cwasm execution path was changed
No third-party dependency was introduced in semantic_core
residual_notes: X6 owns snapshot barrier blocking active IO leases
Future real display and scheduler runtime paths must feed the same semantic benchmark and scheduler objects rather than replacing the X5 integrated evidence contract

validation_commands.command: cargo check -p semantic_core -p artifact_manifest -p contract_core -p target_executor -p osctl

residual_gaps: X6 owns snapshot barrier blocking active IO leases
Real display updates under scheduler load must later provide runtime-backed benchmark evidence through the existing semantic objects
